name: Permissionless IV (Blockworks) — headline of a catch-all of other notable DeFi-relevant conferences not separately covered
edition: Permissionless IV (4th edition; by Blockworks). Catch-all also references TOKEN2049 Singapore 2025 and Consensus 2025 Toronto (CoinDesk).
location: Permissionless IV: Industry City, 220 36th St, Brooklyn, NY, USA. (TOKEN2049: Marina Bay Sands, Singapore; Consensus 2025: Metro Toronto Convention Centre, Toronto, Canada.)
dates: Permissionless IV: June 24-26, 2025 (hackathon June 22-23, 2025). TOKEN2049 Singapore: Oct 1-2, 2025. Consensus 2025 Toronto: May 14-16, 2025 (just before the window; next in-window edition is Consensus 2026).
attendance: Permissionless IV: ~5,000+ attendees expected; 215 confirmed speakers (some sources cite 100+ headline speakers, all ticket tiers sold out). For reference within the catch-all: TOKEN2049 Singapore 2025 ~25,000+ attendees / 500+ side events; Consensus 2025 Toronto ~14,771 attendees from 102 countries, 500 speakers, 1,000+ hackathon devs.
defi_themes: DeFi Renaissance (Blockworks track): RWAs, US capital formation, reshoring DeFi talent/American Dynamism, next-gen DeFi apps
Institutional DeFi & permissioned lending (e.g., Aave's Horizon: institutions borrow stablecoins against tokenized MMFs / US Treasuries while keeping permissionless liquidity)
Real-world asset (RWA) tokenization (grew from ~$5B in 2022 to >$24B by June 2025; crypto's 2nd fastest-growing sector after stablecoins)

Stablecoins as global settlement infrastructure (segmented designs: permissionless/censorship-resistant for crypto-natives vs compliant/bank-issued for institutions; euro stablecoins like Circle EURC surging)
BTCFi — BTC-denominated yield and bringing BTC into EVM (Maple, Core, Lombard, Threshold tBTC)
Restaking / shared security (EigenLayer) and protocol R&D (rollups, ZK, EVM scaling, privacy)
Token design & governance, on-chain treasuries and capital formation
Regulation and the maturing US regulatory environment enabling institutional on-chain finance
DEXs / on-chain trading and perps (Uniswap, Infinex)
Crypto x AI / agents and DePIN (adjacent track)

notable_panels.title: DeFi Renaissance (thematic track)
notable_panels.topic: RWAs, US capital formation, reshoring DeFi talent, and next-generation DeFi applications
notable_panels.speakers: Track-level; featured DeFi founders including Hayden Adams (Uniswap), Stani Kulechov (Aave), Kain Warwick (Infinex)

notable_panels.title: Protocol R&D (thematic track)
notable_panels.topic: Blockchain design, EVM, rollups, scaling, privacy, ZK proofs, restaking/shared security

notable_panels.title: Token Design (thematic track)
notable_panels.topic: Tokenomics, governance, utility mechanics and value accrual

notable_panels.title: Bitcoin / BTCFi (thematic track)
notable_panels.topic: BTC network development, scaling, institutional adoption and BTC-denominated yield (BTCFi)

notable_panels.title: Enterprise (thematic track)
notable_panels.topic: Financial system infrastructure, payments, stablecoins as settlement layer, institutional/commercial applications

notable_speakers.name: Samara Cohen
notable_speakers.affiliation: CIO of ETF & Index Investments / Global Head of Market Development, BlackRock

notable_speakers.name: Eric Adams
notable_speakers.affiliation: Mayor of New York City (civic/keynote appearance)

key_takeaways: Permissionless IV (June 24-26, 2025, Brooklyn) is the most DeFi-centric major in-window event not separately covered, organized by Blockworks; its explicit 'DeFi Renaissance' track framed a thesis that crypto's center of gravity is shifting from infrastructure to DeFi/consumer applications.

Institutional DeFi was the dominant theme: Aave's Horizon (a permissioned Aave V3 instance letting institutions borrow stablecoins against tokenized money-market funds / US Treasuries) was discussed as a template for combining permissionless liquidity with permissioned collateral.
RWA tokenization is treated as crypto's 2nd fastest-growing sector after stablecoins (~$5B in 2022 to >$24B by June 2025), with US Treasuries and MMFs leading as on-chain collateral.

Stablecoins reframed as the settlement backbone linking traditional banking, fintech and DeFi, with a spectrum from decentralized primitives to bank-issued tokens; euro stablecoins (e.g., Circle's EURC) noted as a fast-growing segment.
BTCFi moved from theoretical to real, with BTC-denominated yield (Maple, Core, Lombard) and BTC-into-EVM infrastructure (Threshold tBTC).
Restaking / shared security (EigenLayer) and protocol R&D (ZK, rollups, scaling, privacy) remained core technical themes alongside the DeFi application focus.

Catch-all context: TOKEN2049 Singapore (Oct 1-2, 2025, ~25k attendees) and Consensus by CoinDesk (Toronto, May 14-16, 2025, ~14.8k attendees) carried the same DeFi/stablecoin/RWA/tokenization narrative to broader institutional audiences; at Consensus, stablecoins and tokenization were headline takeaways ('stablecoins took Toronto by storm').

Note: Consensus 2025 Toronto (May 14-16, 2025) falls just before the June 2025 start of the window; it is included for context, and the next in-window edition would be Consensus 2026. Permissionless IV anchors the in_window=true determination.
